posts.text:
Launching a new war.
Ripping health care away from millions.
Unleashing terror on our streets.

This President feels his support collapsing. 

That’s why he is so desperate to suppress the vote.
Did you know that Savannah's St. Patrick's Day Parade is one of the largest in America? Wishing a happy St. Patrick’s Day to all those celebrating in Savannah and across the state of Georgia! ☘️

The spiritual rot of this White House is eating at the core of our nation's highest values.

They are sending Americans to fight and die overseas while kicking people off of health care here at home.
Good news. A judge has just BLOCKED some of Sec. Kennedy’s disastrous attacks on vaccines. This is an important victory for science and for our health, but the fight continues. Keep up the pressure.

War ends with flag draped coffins.
It ends with names etched in gravestones.
And it lives on in the hearts of loved ones left behind.

We can’t lose sight of the true cost of war.
Health insurance should not cost more than your mortgage.

I don’t think families should have to take on a $31,000 deductible in order to find a health care plan they can afford.

We can do better than this.
There’s no money for your health care but they can afford to spend nearly a billion dollars a day on this war?
Billionaires and corporations don't need more tax breaks. Americans need health care.

Despite launching a new war in the Middle East, President Trump has not taken his eye off the ball.
 
He's still determined as ever to complete his goal of kicking millions of Americans off of their health care.

I’m heartbroken by the news that six more American servicemembers have lost their lives in the conflict with Iran. Six families are facing unimaginable loss tonight. We honor their service and keep their loved ones in our prayers.

Republicans in Washington continue to brag about ripping food away from hungry families.  

It’s shameful.

The price of coffee is up 30% over the past year.
nitter.net/senatorwarnock/status/…


Senator Reverend Raphael Warnock (@SenatorWarnock)

Why is President Trump taxing your coffee?

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/SenatorWarnock/status/1954568168178892874#m
If you want to talk about waste, fraud, and abuse, let’s talk about the $11 billion the President just spent on an illegal war that the American people do not want.

The killing of schoolchildren should shock our conscience and cause us to recoil in horror.

A nation whose leaders can so easily shrug off the death of children, no matter the cause, is in need of moral repair. 

There must be accountability and lessons learned from this tragedy. And we must never forget that Iranian children, too, are children of God.

No, Senator. Bigotry is the enemy inside. It sickens the soul that carries it and corrupts the nation that embraces it. Brother, as my Christian faith and yours teaches, LOVE is the only way.
nitter.net/SenTuberville/status/2…


Coach Tommy Tuberville (@SenTuberville)

The enemy is inside the gates.



— https://nitter.net/SenTuberville/status/2032087973810901496#m

I am horrified by the attack at Temple Israel synagogue.

I’m praying for the Jewish community in Michigan.

Antisemitism and hate have no place in America. No one should have to walk into their place of worship fearing violence. Our faith communities should always be places of safety, peace, and refuge.

War is not a joke. It is not a game.

The White House is putting out stuff like this while flag draped coffins come home to broken families.

Is it too much to ask for the commander-in-chief to just take his most solemn and sacred responsibility seriously? Enough.


The White House (@WhiteHouse)

UNDEFEATED.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/WhiteHouse/status/2032115039985881556#m

26 million+ Americans don't have health care. Thousands of veterans are homeless. Children go to bed hungry in this country every single night.

We do not suffer a poverty of resources. We suffer a lack of moral imagination.

Our troops. Veterans. Rural Americans. The middle class. The poor. White, Black, Brown. Young. Old. Middle aged.

EVERYONE is paying the price for Trump’s relentless attack on health care.
nitter.net/abc/status/20317055669…


ABC News (@ABC)

As many as 724,000 service members, their families and veterans may rely on health care at hospitals that face financial vulnerability, partly due to cuts in President Trump's megabill, according to a new analysis. abcnews.link/8NCwSVl




— https://nitter.
